name: Jack Whitehall
bio: Jack Whitehall is an English comedian, actor and television presenter known for posh, self-deprecating observational stand-up. He broke through with sell-out Edinburgh Fringe shows and TV roles as JP in Fresh Meat and Alfie Wickers in Bad Education, then built arena tours and Netflix specials. He has hosted the BRIT Awards multiple times and starred in the travel series Travels with My Father.

timeline.ev: Edinburgh Festival Fringe solo debut Nearly Rebellious, nominated for Best Newcomer
Stars as JP in Channel 4 comedy-drama Fresh Meat
Stars as Alfie Wickers in the BBC Three sitcom Bad Education
Netflix series Travels with My Father premieres
Releases the Settle Down stand-up special on Netflix

accolades: British Comedy Award for Kings of Comedy (2012)
Multiple-time host of the BRIT Awards
Set a Guinness World Record for the highest stand-up gig on a British Airways flight for Comic Relief
